subject: SOCDS 2025/26 Update Week #18 - PreK Orange
text:
Email Ms. Annemarie
5 in a Row
January and February
As explained last week, our themes and school celebrations are very connected in January and February.  These books allow us to nurture our classroom and school community with a focus on inclusion.  This goes beyond accepting differences to celebrating and integrating differences.
We have combined the 5 in a Row books together as well.  I have included a read-aloud below for your reference.
Happy Birthday Martin Luther King
This book summarizes the life of the civil rights leader for children, highlighting his Atlanta childhood, becoming a pastor, leading peaceful protests against segregation (like bus & school segregation), his dream of equality ("I Have a Dream"), and his tragic assassination, all to explain why his birthday is a national holiday celebrating peace and brotherhood
.
Mae Among the Stars
Tells the story of a young girl's dream to travel to space, emphasizing how her parents' encouragement and her own hard work helped her overcome obstacles (like a skeptical teacher) to become the first African American woman in space.
A Computer Called Katherine
Tells the inspiring true story of mathematician
Katherine Johnson
, a math prodigy who overcame racial and gender barriers to become a vital "human computer" at NASA, calculating trajectories for America's first spaceflights, including Alan Shepard's orbit and John Glenn's Earth orbit, ultimately helping land the first man on the Moon.
If I Only Had a Horn
A wonderful journey through the life of Louie Armstrong and his desire to share his music.
Saving the Day
: Garrett Morgan's Life Changing Invention of the Traffic Signal
This story tells the story of how and why Garrett Morgan invented the traffic signal, and the struggles he had along the way.
Your Name is a Song
This book explores the beauty of the names our families gift us and reminds us all to respect and appreciate thenames of our friends - even if we have not heard them before.
A Piece of Home
Explores a families travels to a new community and how they share their own traditions and join in learning about others too
My name is Yoon
This book beautiful describes the beautiful the Korean script (the letters in my name look like dancing birds) and how different the feeling is in English script.
Updated Calendar
Below is a link to our
updated school calendar
.  I added the year to the last few entries for clarification.
Reminder:  SOCDS Closed
Monday, January 19th
In honor of MLK, Jr. Birthday SOCDS will be closed on Monday.
Community Coalition on Race is getting ready for the annual
MLK Luminary Lighting
Town Event.  This Sunday, January 11th, you are invited to the Baird Center to color Luminaries.  The event is from 2-4pm.  You can light up your own neighborhood on Monday January 19th or you can join us all at the
Duck Pond
at 5pm.
If you have any questions or concerns please text (1-646-226-8790) or email learn@socds.com.
Annemarie
Updated School Calendar
Art with Ms. Jenny
Ms. Jenny continued the study of Andy Warhol, exploring the variety of printing techniques he used to create his art pieces.

SOCDS Summer Camp 2025 - March 1st Early Registration
@SOCDSMOMENTS
Logistics
Dress Appropriately for the Weather:
Please dress your child in appropriate gear for the day.  There is no bad weather just poor clothing.
Morning Snack
:  Please send in a morning, and if your child is staying for enrichment please send in an afternoon snack as well.  We generally allow children to choose their food and to listen to their bodies, but if you have any particular preference for what they eat and when, please just label the containers and we will accommodate.  Please remember we are a NUT FREE environment.
Water Bottles
:  Please send in a labeled water bottle with your child each day.  We do make sure that they drink water 
throughout the day.  We have two water fountains which we use to refill the bottles (so they may come home full.)
Parking:
Please remember that Vose Avenue is an active busy street.  Please pull up on the
school side
of Vose in front of the school.  It is safest to go around the block instead of turning around in driveways.  For your child's safety please try to have them leave your car from the sidewalk side of the car.
Please do not block our neighbors driveway, even for a few minutes.
Tuition:
As I receive payments I update the statement and send you a copy.  If you do not receive those from me please let me know.
Hours
Morning Care 7:30-8:30am
Morning Drop off is between 8:30-9am
Afternoon Pick Up is between 3:10- 3:20pm
Extended Day 3:20-5:30/5:45pm
South Orange Country Day School
461 Vose Avenue, South Orange
973.762.6451
You are receiving this email because your child attends SOCDS.  If you no longer wish to receive these updates, please unsubscribe below.
